gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5 / 356)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5 / 356)
text: قُلْتُ لِلرِّضَا (عليه السلام) إِنَّ النَّاسَ رَوَوْا أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) كَانَ إِذَا أَخَذَ فِي طَرِيقٍ رَجَعَ فِي غَيْرِهِ فَهَكَذَا كَانَ يَفْعَلُ قَالَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ فَأَنَا أَفْعَلُهُ كَثِيراً فَافْعَلْهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لِي أَمَا إِنَّهُ أَرْزَقُ لَكَ.

en.hubeali: H 14572 – A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al-Haysam Bin Abu Masrouq Al-Nahdy, from Musa Bin Umar Bin Bazi’e who said:

‘I said to Al-Rezaasws that, ‘The people are reporting that the Rasool Allahsaww, whenever hesaww used to take to a road, would return via another road. So this is what hesaww used to do’. Heasws said: ‘Yes, as Iasws do so quite frequently, so do it’. Then heasws said to me: ‘But rather, it is better for your sustenance’.
en.sarwar: 124. A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al-Haysam Bin Abu Masrouq Al-Nahdy, from Musa Bin Umar Bin Bazi’e who said: ‘I said to Al-Ridha (a.s.) that, ‘The people are reporting that the Rasool Allah (saww), whenever he (saww) used to take to a road, would return via another road. So this is what he (saww) used to do’. He (a.s.) said: ‘Yes, as I (a.s.) do so quite frequently, so do it’. Then he (a.s.) said to me: ‘But rather, it is better for your sustenance’.
